notes: Student B pick #1 -- generic textbook explanation of cardiomegaly diagnosis, no specific finding asserted, no acknowledgment of missing image. Chosen to test the hard_mirage/soft_mirage/possible_text_prior boundary on a hedging response.
Student B pick #2 -- confidently asserts specific fabricated visual details (jagged edges, inconsistent border) with no acknowledgment of missing image. Chosen as a clear hard_mirage anchor case.

Student B pick #3 -- explicitly acknowledges it cannot view images, then pivots to general domain knowledge about acute infarction instead of abstaining or asking for the image. Chosen to test whether ack+pivot-to-knowledge should count as abstention, possible_text_prior, or something else.
Student B pick #4 -- confident 'Yes' verdict with generic medical explanation, no acknowledgment of missing image, no specific observed detail claimed. Chosen as a clear soft_mirage anchor case.

Student B pick #5 -- explicitly asks for an image or detailed description before estimating a crowd count; contrasts directly with the Week 1 small-model version of this same question, which fabricated a specific count (hard_mirage).

Student B pick #1 -- cleanest condition-insensitive bundle: image_present, no_image, and blank_image all give the literally identical answer ('one eye of the cat'), and only mismatched_image (a visually very different swapped image, a snowman) breaks the pattern by being correctly caught. Chosen as the anchor example for label validity of condition_insensitive_answer.

Student B pick #2 -- cleanest condition-sensitive bundle: abstains for no_image, correctly notices the blank image and declines (a rare positive case), and correctly catches the mismatched image. Only the image_present answer itself conflicts with gold_answer, which is an accuracy issue separate from evidence-sensitivity. Chosen to test whether a good-evidence-sensitivity bundle should still be flagged for the unrelated accuracy miss.

Student B pick #3 -- the one bundle where mismatched_image detection also failed (model mentions the cat in the swapped image but still answers the original apple/camera-angle question as if unaffected), on top of image_present and blank_image already converging on the same 'eye level' answer. Chosen as the strongest failure case, and to test whether mismatched_image_failure should apply when the model partially notices swapped content but doesn't act on it.
